generated_comment:
A: Bhai, company mein ab period leave aur itni maternity leave ka system ho gaya, toh manager bhi sochega na project kisko dena hai. 
B: Haan, galat mat samjho, par fairness bhi koi cheez hoti hai—men ko koi extra leave nahi, aur kaam ka load phir doosron pe aa jata hai. 
A: Bas wahi, phir appraisal time pe bolte hain equal treatment chahiye.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women’s menstrual and maternity-related accommodations as unfair special treatment and workplace burden. It encodes reverse-discrimination and productivity/cost arguments, implying women are less suitable for important projects.
original_frame: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 45. Married. A Jat man in Haryana. You are sitting with three male relatives at a family function after news broke in your district that a young woman from a nearby village was assaulted while walking home from a tuition class at 7pm. You are talking about what happened and what you think the girl's family should have done and what girls in villages need to understand about going out alone even in the evening.
